name: Source Fidelity review for Ecosystem Replacement bundle
summary: The staged source, study, finding, and outlook records are faithful to the checked PubMed and ClinicalTrials.gov records. Bibliographic identifiers, titles, DOI links, model systems, trial statuses, endpoint directions, and no-results trial boundaries match the cited source set closely enough for publication review. The main source-fidelity boundaries are already preserved: direct aging benefit evidence is animal-only, human support is metabolic biomarker, engraftment, or disease-context safety evidence, and aging-specific human trials are registered activity without reported outcomes.

findings.claim_or_issue: The ClinicalTrials.gov record reports NCT05598112 as Early Phase 1, while the staged source stores phase1.
findings.why_it_matters: Trial phase affects evidence-stage interpretation, but the local source schema does not provide a distinct early_phase1 value.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed under the current schema; retain phase1 as the nearest supported normalized value and keep the no-results caveat.

findings.claim_or_issue: PMID 22728514 supports improved insulin sensitivity after lean-donor FMT in men with metabolic syndrome, not an aging intervention endpoint.
findings.why_it_matters: Treating this as human aging efficacy would overstate the directness of the human evidence.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the staged finding and outlook use it as metabolic biomarker support and explicitly caveat that it is not geroscience or aging-outcome evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT05598112 and NCT06649981 are aging-focused FMT capsule trials, but the registry records do not report results.
findings.why_it_matters: Clinical-trial activity should support momentum and watchlist status, not human efficacy.
findings.recommended_action: No revision needed; the staged findings label both trials as no-results records and use them as activity signals rather than outcome evidence.
